title: The Three Warm Bedrolls
description: In the root cellar beneath the freight house, three bedrolls are arranged on a raised wooden pallet — two adult-sized and one small, approximately sized for a child of six or seven. All three are still warm to the touch, meaning the occupants left within the last thirty to forty-five minutes. The small bedroll holds a child's cloth doll, left behind in haste. This directly contradicts the bounty dispatch, which describes only two adults — meaning whoever informed on the passengers did not know about the child.
